mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment (UNRATE below 5%) signals a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If during periods when UNRATE is below 5%, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window), reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% the average 20-day S&P 500 return is positive (mean > 0) over any 2-year subperiod, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous three-month growth in both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirms synchronized demand and supply expansion, which historically boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month growth in RSAFS > 0.5% and 3-month growth in INDPRO > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising INDPRO does not produce a statistically significant positive average 20-day forward return (rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year walk-forward period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below the 2017 baseline (index 100), it signals a manufacturing recession. Subsequent increases in INDPRO indicate recovery and lead to positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If during industrial recession (INDPRO < 100) the 5-day S&P 500 return is not positive on average when INDPRO rises from its trough, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window (p < 0.05), reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1000 thousand units signal a depressed housing market, which historically foreshadows broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If in months when HOUST is below 1000 the average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive (mean > 0) over any 3-year period, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous three-month growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month growth in PAYEMS > 0.2% and 3-month decline in UNRATE > 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: If the joint labor signal does not produce a statistically significant positive average 20-day forward return (rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year walk-forward period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing boom (HOUST above 1500), subsequent declines in housing starts signal a peak and impending downturn, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during housing boom (HOUST > 1500) the average 20-day S&P 500 return is positive when HOUST declines from its peak (rank IC < 0.03), reject this hypothesis.
